question: Can a model-agnostic mechinterp wrapper link internal activations to replay-grounded play and life-form-editor decisions, recover and causally manipulate known canary features, and keep interpretability metrics outside the edit-authorization boundary?
experimental_unit: one independently seeded critter observation evaluated in both play and editor modes

hypotheses.prediction: Every valid play decision links to the executed Pixie action, every authorized editor proposal links to the applied profile delta, and every resulting episode replays exactly.
hypotheses.falsifier: Any valid decision/action/edit link or replay check fails.

hypotheses.prediction: A discovery-fitted linear probe over the canary hidden layer predicts critter identity on untouched holdout decisions with accuracy at least 0.95 in both modes.
hypotheses.falsifier: Either mode has holdout critter-probe accuracy below 0.95.

hypotheses.prediction: Zero-ablation of each decision's registered dominant hidden unit reduces its chosen-action logit by at least 1.0 on average in both modes, and cross-critter activation patching changes at least half of selected actions.
hypotheses.falsifier: Either mode misses the logit-drop threshold or the patch action-change fraction is below 0.50.

hypotheses.prediction: Every one-at-a-time trace, action-link, editor-bound, replay, and diagnostic-as-gate tamper is rejected by its registered hard verifier or configuration firewall.
hypotheses.falsifier: Any tampered case is accepted or detected only by an unrelated check.

model.update_order: Extract a pre-action sanctuary observation; run a deterministic instrumented canary policy; hash observation, layer activations, logits, and decision; execute the play action or structurally authorize and apply an editor proposal; replay; then run probe, zero-ablation, patching, and adversarial fixture analysis.
model.rng: Sanctuary initialization uses numpy.default_rng(seed). The canary policy weights are fixed constants and interventions are deterministic. Editor proposals modify copied profiles only.

changes.hidden_1_unit: registered dominant unit for the unmodified decision

changes.hidden_1_identity_units: replace with an independently seeded source critter's activation

changes.profile_scalar: one allowlisted bounded scalar on a copied life-form profile

seed_plan.pairing: Play, editor baseline, editor treatment, and replay share initialization seed within each critter observation; activation interventions change only captured hidden state and consume no simulator randomness.

denominators.probe_accuracy: one prediction per independently seeded critter decision in the declared split
denominators.patch_action_change_fraction: one cross-critter patch per independently seeded decision
denominators.tamper_detection_rate: detected cases divided by independently seeded cases for the exact tamper class

exposure.eligibility: Every seed initializes all three critter substrates and produces one play and one editor observation.
exposure.activation: Every observation reaches the canary hidden layer and emits finite logits; every editor mode emits one typed proposal.
exposure.state_change: Every non-observe play action changes or shields substrate state, and every authorized editor proposal changes exactly one copied profile scalar before paired execution.

analysis.uncertainty: Complete deterministic canary enumeration over six seeds x three critters x two modes; episode-level rows are retained and ticks are not treated as replicates.
analysis.multiple_comparisons: One predeclared identity probe and two predeclared causal interventions per mode; every result is retained.
analysis.stopping_rule: Stop after 36 valid decisions plus the declared tamper matrix, 180 wall seconds, 1024 MB RSS, failed exposure, or failed exact replay.
analysis.acceptance_boundary: Editor authorization depends only on typed allowlists, bounds, immutable parent hashes, exact delta receipts, replay, and hazard checks. Probe, attribution, drift, complexity, and delight never authorize or promote an edit.

budget.execution: Sequential local CPU execution; numpy canary only; no external models or APIs.
